title: Khlii & Eggs (Chapman Version)

overview.description: A Moroccan-inspired breakfast of warmly spiced beef and eggs, adapted using fresh beef instead of traditional preserved khlii while keeping the flavours true to the original.

method: Heat the olive oil in a frying pan over a medium-high heat.
Brown the beef well on all sides.
Reduce the heat and stir in the garlic.
Add the cumin, paprika, coriander, ginger and turmeric.
Season with salt and black pepper.
Spread the beef evenly across the pan.
Crack the eggs over the beef.
Cover with a lid and cook until the egg whites are set and the yolks are cooked to your liking.
Finish with chopped parsley before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Traditional khlii is preserved meat that can be stored for months without refrigeration and is often served with eggs for breakfast in Morocco.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How Moroccan spices transform a simple breakfast and how to cook eggs gently over a savoury base.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: Introduces Moroccan breakfast flavours without requiring specialist preserved meat that's difficult to source in the UK.
chapman-tips: Traditional khlii can be substituted if you ever make or buy it.
Don't overcook the beef—it should stay tender.
Batbout is the perfect bread for soaking up the runny yolk.
A pinch of ras el hanout can be added for extra warmth.
notes.batch-cooking: Not suitable for freezing once assembled.
notes.leftovers: The cooked beef can be reheated gently and topped with freshly cooked eggs.
